name: Senda del Oso and Puertos de Marabio Loop
name_original: Puertos de Marabio – circular desde Proeza
description: This difficult 49.7-mile road cycling loop combines the Senda del Oso's green valleys with the challenging climbs of Puertos de Marabio.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Proaza navigate a landscape shaped by the Trubia River, featuring verdant scenery and mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by impressive gorges and extensive forests, providing varied backdrops for cycling. Riders encounter challenging ascents and scenic routes through valleys and meadows. This area offers diverse physical features for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Proaza The most popular road cycling route is San Lorenzo, Somiedo, and Ventana Passes Loop, a 62.9 miles (101.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses multiple mountain passes, offering sustained climbing. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Ventana from San Martín de Teverga, a difficult 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route features a significant ascent to Puerto de Ventana, providing panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Senda del Oso and Puertos de Marabio Loop, a 49.7 miles (80.0 km) trail leading through the Senda del Oso greenway and mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours. Road cycling around Proaza is defined by lush green landscapes, river valleys, and mountainous ascents. The network offers options for both challenging climbs and more accessible sections, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Proaza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Proaza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling routes in Proaza?
A: Proaza is known for its challenging terrain. While there are no easy road cycling routes, you'll find 6 moderate routes and 24 difficult routes, many featuring significant ascents and mountainous landscapes. The region is particularly suited for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Proaza?
A: Yes, while many road cycling routes are challenging, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6736914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent family-friendly option. This green route, following an old mining railway line, offers flat, paved sections free of motor traffic. Bike rentals, including child trailers and baby seats, are readily available, making it ideal for families with young children.

Q: Which road cycling routes offer panoramic views of the mountains?
A: Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067293463/puerto-de-ventana-from-san-martin-de-teverga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Ventana from San Martín de Teverga&lt;/a&gt; feature significant ascents that provide breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. The mountainous terrain throughout Proaza ensures many routes offer spectacular vistas.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Proaza?
A: Yes, Proaza offers several loop routes for road cyclists. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069359431/senda-del-oso-and-puertos-de-marabio-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso and Puertos de Marabio Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines the greenway with mountainous terrain. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067410664/san-lorenzo-somiedo-and-ventana-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo, Somiedo, and Ventana Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, traversing multiple mountain passes.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see along road cycling routes in Proaza?
A: Along the routes, you can encounter impressive natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas on the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular gorge carved by the Trubia River. The region is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/297709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt;, known for its impressive gorges and stalactites.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Proaza has a rich medieval heritage. You can find cultural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5520289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Tower of Proaza&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the medieval village of Banduxu and the González Tuñón Palace, offering glimpses into its history.

Q: What is the Senda del Oso and what can I expect there?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6736914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; (Bear Trail) is a prominent green route in Proaza, following an old mining railway line. It's known for its varied terrain, from flat, paved sections suitable for families to more challenging parts with spectacular mountain scenery. A major highlight is the Proaza Bear Enclosure, where you can observe rescued Cantabrian brown bears.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Proaza?
A: The road cycling routes in Proaza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the lush green landscapes, the challenging ascents, and the diverse terrain that caters to different ability levels, particularly those seeking a rewarding climb.

Q: Is there a particularly challenging climb for advanced road cyclists in Proaza?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/559587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de l'Angliru&lt;/a&gt; is a legendary road cycling challenge nearby. It features an average gradient of 8.2 percent over 8.7 kilometers, reaching an altitude of 845 meters, making it a formidable test for advanced riders.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Proaza area?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes available around Proaza, offering a wide range of distances and elevation gains. These routes navigate the region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountainous ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect while road cycling in Proaza?
A: Road cycling in Proaza offers a diverse and picturesque experience. You'll ride through lush green landscapes shaped by the Trubia River, encounter impressive gorges like Peñas Juntas, and traverse extensive forests, including centuries-old chestnut and beech trees. The routes also feature spectacular mountains, valleys, and meadows.
tips:
In the Cantabrian mountain range we find ourselves rolling through Puertos de Marabio, a large mountain pass of great hydrogeological value due to the existence of sinkholes and blind valleys. This karstic system covers 1,225 hectares of the Principality of Asturias.
For road bike lovers, through Puertos de Marabio, we find 3 ascent variants:
- San Pedro, with a length of 19.40 km and a drop of 910 m. An average slope of 4.69% and a maximum of 18.
- Villamayor, with a length of 8.70 km and a drop of 620 m. An average slope of 7.12% and a maximum of 21.
- Prado, with a length of 11.90 km and a drop of 620 m. An average slope of 5.21% and a maximum of 16.
A pleasure for road bike lovers to be able to ride on these dream roads of the Las Ubiñas Natural Park.

Puertos de Marabio is at 1,225 and has 3 beautiful ascent variants that I am going to detail below;
- San Pedro, with a length of 19.40 km and a drop of 910 m. An average slope of 4.69% and a maximum of 18.
- Villamayor, with a length of 8.70 km and a drop of 620 m. An average slope of 7.12% and a maximum of 21.
- Prado, with a length of 11.90 km and a drop of 620 m. An average slope of 5.21% and a maximum of 16.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Senda del Oso and Puertos de Marabio Loop by road bike?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling loop, covering approximately 80 km, generally takes around 5 hours to complete. However, your actual duration may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for road cycling. While the Senda del Oso section (when ridden on adjacent roads) offers mostly gentle gradients, the Puertos de Marabio climb significantly increases the challenge. Expect substantial ascents, with some segments reaching steep gradients of over 13%, requiring good training and appropriate climbing gears.

faqs.question: Where are the best places to park for this loop?
faqs.answer: For this loop, a good starting point is near Proaza, as it offers access to the Senda del Oso and is well-positioned for the full circuit. Parking options are available in and around the villages along the Senda del Oso, such as Proaza or Entrago, which also serve as approaches to Puertos de Marabio.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and road surfaces can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse experience. The Senda del Oso portion, typically ridden on adjacent main roads like the AS-228, features smooth, paved surfaces through verdant valleys. However, the Puertos de Marabio section, while fully asphalted, can have mixed road conditions, with some parts described as bumpy, cracked, and occasionally having loose gravel, especially near the summit and on descents. It's a quiet route with very little traffic.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the Senda del Oso and Puertos de Marabio Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll experience contrasting Asturian landscapes. Along the Senda del Oso, you'll ride through lush river valleys, past dramatic gorges, and have the unique opportunity to see Cantabrian brown bears in a semi-natural enclosure near Proaza. The former railway infrastructure, including tunnels and bridges, adds historical charm. The Puertos de Marabio section offers fantastic, open views across the rolling Asturian countryside, especially from the Summit of the Puertos de Marabio after a challenging climb.

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Oso and Puertos de Marabio Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Senda del Oso greenway itself is generally dog-friendly (on leash), this specific road cycling loop incorporates main roads and challenging mountain passes. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are comfortable with long distances, road cycling environments, and that you adhere to local regulations, especially within the Las Ubiñas-La Mesa Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is typically during the spring (late April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be pleasant but may have hotter temperatures, especially on the exposed mountain passes. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, making the Puertos de Marabio section impassable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within Las Ubiñas-La Mesa Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route or to access the general areas of the Las Ubiñas-La Mesa Natural Park through which it passes. However, always respect local regulations regarding nature conservation, waste disposal, and wildlife, particularly concerning the Cantabrian brown bears.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop incorporates significant portions of the Vía Verde Senda del Oso and also crosses sections of the [CIMA AS22] Marabio * Prado and [CIMA AS22] Marabio * San Pedro. You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Sendero Histórico: Asturias and Asturias Interior (Etapa 15).

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding the descent from Puertos de Marabio?
faqs.answer: The descent from Puertos de Marabio demands caution. While rewarding, some sections can be bumpy, cracked, and have loose gravel. It's crucial to maintain a safe speed, be attentive to the road surface, and ensure your bike is in excellent condition, especially your brakes, for a safe and enjoyable descent.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, particularly along the Senda del Oso section, you'll find cafes and restaurants in the small villages you pass through, such as Proaza. These are good spots to refuel and rest. The Puertos de Marabio section is more remote, so plan to carry sufficient water and snacks for that part of the ride.

waypoints.name: Summit of the Puertos de Marabio
